content.summaryText: Unconscious Airway Management in Airway Emergencies focuses on rapid airway patency check and oxygenation support. Primary objective: Initiate emergency call protocol.

content.questionText: In Unconscious Airway Management, which action best supports 'Initiate emergency call protocol'?
content.explanation: Correct answers prioritize patient safety, reassessment, and timely escalation.

options.text: Open airway, call support, and monitor breathing continuously
Delaying escalation while obstruction signs are worsening
Delay reassessment until symptoms are severe
Use treatment decisions without baseline documentation

content.summaryText: Immediate priority: open airway, call support, and monitor breathing continuously. Practical emphasis: Start CPR sequence.

content.questionText: Escalation is required when stridor, cyanosis, decreasing consciousness, or no airflow.
content.explanation: Early escalation reduces preventable deterioration and delays in definitive care.

content.sentence:
Start with <1> to establish baseline status.
Use <2> to deliver standardized intervention.
Document <3> to support continuity and quality review.
